message:
fix: use Bun.which() for cross-platform PATH lookup, validate explicit cliCommand

1. Replaced Bun.spawnSync(["which", tool]) with Bun.which(tool). The
   `which` command doesn't exist on Windows, breaking the Windows build
   target. Bun.which() is cross-platform (Linux/macOS/Windows), returns
   string|null synchronously, and avoids spawning a subprocess per check.

2. Explicit cliCommand values (from config or env) are now validated
   against PATH via Bun.which(). Previously, resolveCliCommand("bad")
   returned "bad" directly — the error only surfaced at Bun.spawn time
   with a confusing OS-level error. Now returns null, and the caller
   gets a clear "No CLI LLM tool found" message.

3. Removed unverified codex --quiet flag. Only claude -p is verified to
   work with stdin piping. Other tools use empty flags as a safe default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
